message:
feat(resolv): port libresolv ns_sprintrr + ns_sprintrrf (DNS RR formatter) (bd-cmimx)

Add the DNS resource-record text formatters. Output format is
BIND zone-file syntax: "name TTL class type rdata".

  - ns_sprintrrf(msg, msglen, name, class, type, ttl, rdata, rdlen,
    name_ctx, origin, buf, buflen) is the workhorse. TTL is formatted
    via our existing ns_format_ttl. Class: 1=IN, 2=CS, 3=CH, 4=HS,
    else "CLASSn". Type: 1=A, 2=NS, 5=CNAME, 6=SOA, 12=PTR, 15=MX,
    16=TXT, 28=AAAA, 33=SRV, else "TYPEn". Per-type rdata:
      - A    -> dotted-quad
      - AAAA -> std::net::Ipv6Addr Display (RFC 5952 canonical)
      - NS / CNAME / PTR -> dn_expand of the rdata bytes
      - MX   -> "pref " + dn_expand(name)
      - TXT  -> sequence of length-prefixed quoted strings with
                printable bytes preserved and \DDD escapes for
                non-printables; '"' and '\\' get backslash escapes
      - everything else -> RFC 3597 generic: "\\# rdlen hexbytes"
    Returns chars written or -1 on overflow / parse error.
  - ns_sprintrr(handle, rr, name_ctx, origin, buf, buflen) is the
    convenience wrapper that pulls msg / name / class / type / ttl /
    rdata / rdlen out of a parsed CNsMsg+CNsRr pair and forwards.

Tests: 8 abi tests covering A, AAAA, MX (with compressed-name
pointer inside the same synthetic message), TXT (with internal
quote escaping), unknown-type RFC 3597 fallback, buffer overflow,
malformed A rdata length, and end-to-end ns_sprintrr through
ns_initparse + ns_parserr.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-clk1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc time.h clock/sleep diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 4 functions: clock_gettime (5 clocks: REALTIME/MONOTONIC/PROCESS_CPUTIME_ID/THREAD_CPUTIME_ID/BOOTTIME), clock_getres, nanosleep (3 short durations + 3 invalid edges), clock_nanosleep (TIMER_ABSTIME with past wake). 6 tests, 0 divergences against glibc.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T10:45:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T10:45:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T10:45:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; 0 divergences.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clock","conformance","differential","time"]}
 {"id":"bd-clua","title":"resolv: add host-parity ABI probes for synchronous-only async-DNS wrappers","description":"Capture concrete glibc behavior for getaddrinfo_a/gai_error/gai_cancel/gai_suspend degenerate cases in focused ABI probes so future resolver stub work is grounded in observed host semantics instead of assumptions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:27:54.963463136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:06:13.918812607Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:06:13.916727971Z","close_reason":"Added host-parity getaddrinfo_a mode matrix probes and aligned invalid-mode degenerate behavior with observed host libc semantics.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-clxf1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc string.h strcoll + strxfrm diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 2 functions: strcoll (12 string pairs in C locale, sign comparison since magnitude is implementation-defined), strxfrm (size-query then transform; verifies strcmp(xfrm(a), xfrm(b)).sign == strcoll(a, b).sign for both impls — the POSIX consistency invariant). 3 tests, 0 divergences against glibc.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T11:30:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T11:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T11:30:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; 0 divergences.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["collation","conformance","differential","string"]}
+{"id":"bd-cmimx","title":"PORT: ns_sprintrr + ns_sprintrrf (libresolv RR formatter, common types + RFC3597 fallback)","description":"libresolv ns_sprintrr(handle, rr, name_ctx, origin, buf, buflen) is a wrapper that reads fields from a CNsRr/CNsMsg pair and forwards to ns_sprintrrf. ns_sprintrrf(msg, msglen, name, class, type, ttl, rdata, rdlen, name_ctx, origin, buf, buflen) formats one DNS resource record in BIND zone-file syntax: "name TTL class type rdata". TTL via ns_format_ttl. Class: 1=IN, 2=CS, 3=CH, 4=HS, else "CLASSn". Type: A=1, NS=2, CNAME=5, PTR=12, MX=15, AAAA=28, TXT=16; else "TYPEn". Per-type rdata: A=dotted quad, AAAA=inet_ntop(AF_INET6), NS/CNAME/PTR=dn_expand from rdata, MX="pref name", TXT="\"string\" \"string\"" with backslash-escaping. Unknown types use RFC 3597 generic notation: "\\# rdlen hexbytes". Returns chars written or -1 on overflow / parse error. Both handle NULL buf via -1.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:26:25.156683691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:26:25.156683691Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cmiv","title":"EPIC: Implement real DNS resolution for getaddrinfo","description":"getaddrinfo() currently only resolves:
1. Numeric IP addresses (127.0.0.1, ::1)
2. Hostnames found in /etc/hosts

It does NOT perform actual DNS queries. This means programs like wget, curl (for non-localhost URLs), dig, nslookup, and any network client that resolves hostnames will fail.

OPTIONS:
A) Delegate to host libc's getaddrinfo for non-numeric, non-/etc/hosts hostnames
B) Implement a minimal DNS stub resolver (send UDP query to nameserver from /etc/resolv.conf)
C) Both: try our resolver first, fall back to host

Option A is fastest to implement and most reliable. Option B gives us full independence from host libc.

ALREADY DONE: /etc/hosts lookup works, numeric IP parsing works, freeaddrinfo correctly frees our Box-allocated structures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.603558212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T18:43:31.403160236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T18:43:31.402959135Z","close_reason":"Already implemented. getaddrinfo has a full native DNS stub resolver: udp_dns_query() sends A/AAAA queries to /etc/resolv.conf nameservers via SYS_socket/SYS_sendto/SYS_recvfrom with configurable timeout/retry. ResolverConfig parses nameservers, search domains, ndots. Resolution chain: numeric -> /etc/hosts -> DNS -> repair fallback. 46 resolv tests pass. Previously verified when closing bd-zh1y.7 and bd-1y7.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cmiv.1","title":"Add host libc delegation for DNS-requiring getaddrinfo calls","description":"When getaddrinfo receives a hostname that:
1. Is NOT a numeric IP address
2. Is NOT found in /etc/hosts

Delegate to the host libc's getaddrinfo via our ELF resolver. The host's getaddrinfo will perform the actual DNS query.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. resolve_host_symbol_raw("getaddrinfo") to get host's function pointer
2. Call host_getaddrinfo(node, service, hints, res) 
3. Return the host's result directly (the addrinfo structs are heap-allocated by the host)
4. In freeaddrinfo, detect whether the addrinfo was allocated by us (Box) or host (malloc) and free accordingly

TRICKY PART: freeaddrinfo must distinguish our Box-allocated addrinfos from host-malloc-allocated ones. Could use a sentinel field or check if the pointer is in our Box allocation range.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.811880807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:30:57.450087855Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:30:57.448613314Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cmiv.1","depends_on_id":"bd-cmiv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:15.811880807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-cnf1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc sysconf+pathconf+confstr diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 4 functions: sysconf (12 common names cross-checked), pathconf (/, _PC_NAME_MAX/_PC_PATH_MAX), fpathconf (/dev/null), confstr (_CS_PATH size+content, _CS_GNU_LIBC_VERSION zero-match). Discovered DISC-SYSCONF-001: fl returns POSIX min for _SC_CHILD_MAX/_SC_STREAM_MAX while glibc queries live rlimit. Discovered DISC-SYSCONF-002: fl returns POSIX min for _PC_LINK_MAX (127) while glibc queries actual filesystem (65000 on ext4). Both documented; bd-cnf2 opened. 8 tests, 0 substantive divergences.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T15:30:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; documented divergences logged.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","sysconf"]}
